raw_text:
CLINICAL NOTE - Date: 2026-01-15

Patient: 45-year-old African American female presenting for routine diabetes follow-up.

HPI: Patient was diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes mellitus 4 years ago. She reports intermittent adherence to metformin due to GI side effects. She monitors her blood glucose 2-3 times per week with fasting readings typically 160-200 mg/dL. She reports increased thirst and nocturia x2 over the past month. She denies visual changes, numbness, or tingling in extremities. She also has a history of hypertension diagnosed in 2019, currently managed with amlodipine 5mg daily.

Vitals: BP 142/88 mmHg, HR 78 bpm, Temp 98.4F, RR 16, SpO2 98% RA. Weight 187 lbs, Height 5'5", BMI 31.1.

Physical Exam: Alert and oriented. Heart RRR, no murmurs. Lungs CTA bilaterally. Abdomen soft, non-tender. Lower extremities without edema. Monofilament sensation intact bilaterally. Fundoscopic exam unremarkable.

Labs: HbA1c 8.2% (up from 7.6% six months ago), fasting glucose 180 mg/dL, creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, eGFR >90, total cholesterol 218 mg/dL, LDL 138 mg/dL, triglycerides 195 mg/dL, microalbumin/creatinine ratio 22 mg/g.

Assessment and Plan:
1. Type 2 diabetes mellitus - suboptimal control. Continue metformin 1000mg BID, add empagliflozin 10mg daily. Reinforce dietary counseling and refer to diabetes educator. Recheck HbA1c in 3 months.
2. Hypertension - borderline controlled. Increase amlodipine to 10mg daily. Home BP monitoring recommended.
3. Dyslipidemia - initiate atorvastatin 20mg daily.
4. Follow-up in 3 months with repeat labs.
